abstract: Fires in smart cities represent a critical threat to urban infrastructure and public safety, necessitating rapid and reliable detection to prevent catastrophic loss. Traditional sensor-based methods often struggle with response times and accuracy, particularly in large-scale or outdoor environments. To address these limitations, this paper introduces the Smart Fire Detection System (SFDS), an improved real-time approach leveraging the state-of-the-art YOLOv8 deep learning algorithm. By focusing on the extraction of fire-specific visual features, the SFDS enhances detection precision and minimizes false alarms compared to conventional methods. The proposed architecture is integrated into a multi-layered smart city framework comprising IoT, Fog, Cloud, and Application layers. This structure facilitates low-latency data processing at the edge while utilizing cloud resources for extensive data management, ensuring a swift response to emergencies. Experimental results demonstrate that the SFDS achieves state-of-the-art performance, reaching a high precision rate of 97.1% across all classes. Beyond fire safety, the system’s versatile design allows it to be adapted for monitoring other urban hazards such as flooding or gas leaks, making it a robust solution for intelligent security and forest fire management.
